changed_functions.name: CheckSupportedHardware
key_changes: The only behavioral difference in the whole diff. CheckSupportedHardware reads a hardware version value (device context +0x44) and a PCI revision value (+0x48) and dispatches on version/revision against the threshold 0xF0. The single reject sentinel 'mov r14d, 0C0000260h' (STATUS_DEVICE_DATA_ERROR) exists at address 0x140013B92 in BOTH builds.

Across all reachable version/revision paths the outcomes are identical between builds EXCEPT the combination {version 4, revision < 0xF0}: the unpatched build (0x14001373C) lets this case fall through the version dispatch into the generic reject at 0x140013B92 (returns STATUS_DEVICE_DATA_ERROR); the patched build (0x1400136A4) handles it directly in the version-4 branch (0x1400139AD) and returns STATUS_SUCCESS.
Direction is a RELAXATION: the patched driver accepts hardware the unpatched driver rejected. This is a device-support/compatibility gate change, not a safety gate being added.
Called only from OnDeviceAdd (WDF EvtDriverDeviceAdd) at 0x1400012DE (unpatched) / 0x1400012D4 (patched). It is not called from OnPrepareHardware.
changed_functions.security_notes: Not security relevant. The version and revision values are hardware-identity values read from PCI configuration space / device enumeration by GetHardwareVersion and GetHardwareRevision; they are not controllable through any software (IOCTL / user-mode) input path. No memory-safety primitive is introduced or removed. The change only alters which NTSTATUS CheckSupportedHardware returns for one hardware-identity combination, and in the direction of accepting more hardware (previously rejected) rather than rejecting it. The prior claim that the patch ADDS a rejection of version 5 with revision < 0xF0 is incorrect: version 5 with revision < 0xF0 returns success in both builds, and the actual changed cell is version 4 with revision < 0xF0 (reject in unpatched, accept in patched).
changed_functions.assembly_diff:
Unpatched CheckSupportedHardware @ 0x14001373C, version 4 handling (ecx = 0xF0):
0x140013A3B  cmp eax, 4
0x140013A3E  jnz short loc_140013AB7
0x140013A40  cmp [rdi+48h], ecx        ; revision vs 0xF0
0x140013A43  jb short loc_140013A79    ; revision < 0xF0
0x140013A79  cmp eax, 4
0x140013A7C  jnz short loc_140013AB7
0x140013A7E  cmp [rdi+48h], ecx
0x140013A81  jb short loc_140013AB7    ; revision < 0xF0 -> dispatch continues
; version==4 fails remaining version tests (5,6,5,7) -> generic reject:
0x140013B92  mov r14d, 0C0000260h      ; STATUS_DEVICE_DATA_ERROR (REJECT)

Patched CheckSupportedHardware @ 0x1400136A4, version 4 handling:
0x1400139AD  cmp eax, 4
0x1400139B0  jnz loc_140013A49
0x1400139B6  cmp dword ptr [rdi+48h], 0F0h
0x1400139BD  jnb short loc_140013A04   ; revision >= 0xF0 -> success trace 0x18
; revision < 0xF0 falls through -> success trace 0x17:
0x1400139FA  mov edx, 17h
0x1400139FF  jmp loc_140013B7C         ; WPP_SF_ds trace then SUCCESS tail

Error sentinel identical in both builds:
; unpatched  0x140013B92  mov r14d, 0C0000260h
; patched    0x140013B92  mov r14d, 0C0000260h

changed_functions.name: GetCurrentDmaTransferLeft
key_changes: Register renaming (rsi→r14, r14→rbp, r13→rsi) due to compiler differences
ETW trace GUID rename (WPP hash change)

changed_functions.name: ControllerProcessInterrupts
key_changes: Address changes due to binary layout differences

changed_functions.name: DmaInitSw
key_changes: Compiler restructuring of WPP trace guard checks

changed_functions.name: FillDmaTxLliBuffer
key_changes: Core DMA LLI buffer fill logic unchanged

changed_functions.name: FillDmaRxLliBuffer
key_changes: Core DMA LLI buffer fill logic unchanged

changed_functions.name: GetHardwareVersion
key_changes: Hardware ID matching table and logic unchanged

non_security_changes.entry_point: OnDeviceAdd (WDF EvtDriverDeviceAdd callback) - called during device add via PnP manager
non_security_changes.change_class: Hardware-support / device-compatibility gate relaxation (not security relevant)
non_security_changes.description: CheckSupportedHardware reads a hardware version value (device context +0x44) and a PCI revision value (+0x48) and runs a version/revision dispatch against the threshold 0xF0. Tracing every reachable path in both builds, all outcomes are identical except the combination {version 4, revision < 0xF0}: the unpatched build (0x14001373C) rejects it (falls into the generic STATUS_DEVICE_DATA_ERROR sentinel at 0x140013B92), while the patched build (0x1400136A4) accepts it (version-4 branch at 0x1400139AD returns STATUS_SUCCESS). The patch therefore RELAXES the gate, accepting hardware the unpatched driver refused. The STATUS_DEVICE_DATA_ERROR sentinel at 0x140013B92 is present identically in both builds.
non_security_changes.why_not_security: The version and revision values are hardware-identity values read from PCI configuration space / device enumeration; they are not influenced by any software (IOCTL or user-mode) input. No memory-safety primitive is introduced or removed. The direction is a relaxation (more hardware accepted), which is the opposite of a safety gate being added. There is no demonstrable reachable impact.
non_security_changes.correction_of_prior_claim: The prior draft claimed the patch ADDS rejection of version 5 with revision < 0xF0 and rated it Medium. That is incorrect on three counts: (1) version 5 with revision < 0xF0 returns success in BOTH builds; (2) the actual changed cell is version 4 with revision < 0xF0; (3) the direction is reject->accept (relaxation), not accept->reject (hardening). The 'mov r14d, 0xC0000260' error value is not new in the patched build.
non_security_changes.evidence:
Unpatched (0x14001373C), version 4 with revision < 0xF0 falls through to the generic reject:
0x140013A3B  cmp eax, 4
0x140013A3E  jnz short loc_140013AB7
0x140013A40  cmp [rdi+48h], ecx        ; ecx = 0xF0
0x140013A43  jb short loc_140013A79    ; revision < 0xF0
0x140013A7E  cmp [rdi+48h], ecx
0x140013A81  jb short loc_140013AB7    ; continues, version 4 fails all remaining version tests
0x140013B92  mov r14d, 0C0000260h      ; STATUS_DEVICE_DATA_ERROR

Patched (0x1400136A4), version 4 with revision < 0xF0 returns success:
0x1400139AD  cmp eax, 4
0x1400139B0  jnz loc_140013A49
0x1400139B6  cmp dword ptr [rdi+48h], 0F0h
0x1400139BD  jnb short loc_140013A04   ; revision >= 0xF0 -> success
0x1400139FA  mov edx, 17h              ; revision < 0xF0 -> success trace
0x1400139FF  jmp loc_140013B7C

summary: This binary diff between two versions of the Intel LPSS2 I2C controller driver (ialpss2i_i2c.sys) contains 99 changed functions, 98 of which show only ETW/WPP tracing infrastructure changes (GUID hash renames, Template_* -> McTemplateK0* naming, WPP_GLOBAL_Control relocation) and compiler optimization differences (register allocation, branch-to-branchless transforms). The single behavioral change is in CheckSupportedHardware (similarity 0.7273): the patched build accepts an Intel I2C controller enumerated as hardware version 4 with PCI revision ID below 0xF0, a combination the unpatched build rejected with STATUS_DEVICE_DATA_ERROR. This is a hardware-support/compatibility gate relaxation, reached only via hardware-identity values (PCI version/revision) that are not attacker-controllable through software input, and it introduces no memory-safety primitive. There is no security-relevant change in this patch. A prior draft's claim of a Medium-severity added safety gate for version 5 with revision < 0xF0 was incorrect: that combination returns success in both builds, the actually-changed cell is version 4, and the change direction is reject->accept rather than accept->reject.
